LanguageData.UB_Tribal_AspiringHunter.title: Aspiring hunter
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_AspiringHunter.description: [PAWN_nameDef] greatly admired the hunters of [PAWN_possessive] tribe. [PAWN_pronoun] would often shirk [PAWN_possessive] chores to sneak away and practice with [PAWN_possessive] toy bow and arrow.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ChildWarrior.title: Child warrior
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ChildWarrior.description: After many years of war with a much stronger tribe, [PAWN_nameDef]’s people were all but wiped out. With very few able-bodied adults left to fight, the children of the tribe, including [PAWN_nameDef], were forced to take up arms to defend their homes and loved ones.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ApprenticeHealer.title: Apprentice healer
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ApprenticeHealer.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was chosen to be trained as a healer. [PAWN_possessive] tribe’s kindly medicine woman was a skilled and patient teacher, and taught [PAWN_nameDef] how to tend to the sick, as well as how to grow healing herbs.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_RaisedByWolves.title: Raised by wolves
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_RaisedByWolves.description: Whether on purpose or by accident, [PAWN_nameDef] was somehow seperated from [PAWN_possessive] parents as a baby. A pack of wolves came across the helpless infant and adopted [PAWN_objective] as one of their own. It wasn’t until many years later that [PAWN_nameDef] was finally found by a tribe of [PAWN_possessive] own species. By then, it was too late for [PAWN_objective] to fully learn many fundamental social skills.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ApprenticeWeaver.title: Apprentice weaver
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ApprenticeWeaver.description: [PAWN_nameDef] dexterously spun loose fibres into thread, which [PAWN_possessive] teacher then wove into clothing and other textiles for the tribe. [PAWN_nameDef] favourite part of [PAWN_possessive] work was helping to pick and prepare the plants for making the colourful dyes that were used for the thread.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_AnimalSlayer.title: Banished child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_AnimalSlayer.description: [PAWN_nameDef]’s actions led to the death of one [PAWN_possessive] tribe’s most sacred animals. While normally the punishment for such a crime was death, the tribe elders’ took [PAWN_nameDef]’s young age into account, and instead sent [PAWN_objective] to live with another tribe.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CreepyChild.title: Creepy child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CreepyChild.description: The other children of [PAWN_nameDef]’s tribe always did their best to avoid [PAWN_objective]. Even the adults gave [PAWN_objective] a wide berth. There was just something off about [PAWN_objective]. [PAWN_nameDef] spent most of [PAWN_possessive] time alone, throwing rocks at defenseless animals.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_TwigCollector.title: Twig collector
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_TwigCollector.description: [PAWN_nameDef] helped to collect twigs and branches to keep [PAWN_possessive] tribe’s fires lit. [PAWN_pronoun] enjoyed stacking the twigs up into little forts and tepees, discovering how to make sturdier and sturdier versions of the tiny structures.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_Winterborn.title: Winterborn
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_Winterborn.description: According to the beliefs of [PAWN_nameDef]’s tribe, children who are born during harsh winters and survive are destined to become mighty warriors. [PAWN_nameDef] was born during just such a winter.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ExileChild.title: Child of an exile
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ExileChild.description: [PAWN_nameDef]’s mother was exiled from her village while she was pregnant with [PAWN_objective]. She raised [PAWN_nameDef] alone as best she could, teaching [PAWN_objective] how to survive in the wilderness without the support of a tribe.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_TreeClimber.title: Tree climber
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_TreeClimber.description: [PAWN_nameDef] loved climbing trees more than anything else. [PAWN_pronoun] could climb higher than any of the other children in [PAWN_possessive] tribe, and enjoyed spending time watching the world below from up in the branches of the tallest trees.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_DollMaker.title: Doll maker
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_DollMaker.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was often put in charge of watching over the younger children. To entertain them, [PAWN_pronoun] learned to make dolls out of dried leaves. [PAWN_possessive] dolls were so popular that [PAWN_pronoun] was eventually given other, more valuable, materials to make them with.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_TroubleMaker.title: Troublemaker
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_TroubleMaker.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was constantly up to no good. Stealing and hiding things, harassing animals, and taunting the other children, [PAWN_pronoun] always found a way to make a nuisance of [PAWN_objective]self. As punishment, [PAWN_nameDef] was often sent to bed with no supper, but [PAWN_pronoun] would simply sneak out of bed and cook something for [PAWN_objective]self.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_BaitCollector.title: Bait collector
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_BaitCollector.description: [PAWN_nameDef] dug in the dirt to collect worms and grubs for [PAWN_possessive] tribe’s fishers to use as bait. When [PAWN_pronoun] was particularly successful, [PAWN_pronoun] would feed some of the spare bugs to the birds, daydreaming about what it might be like to fly as [PAWN_pronoun] watched them.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_MudMixer.title: Mud mixer
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_MudMixer.description: [PAWN_nameDef] helped to mix the mud and clay mixture that [PAWN_possessive] tribe’s builders used to make bricks. The builders often allowed [PAWN_objective] to watch them work, teaching [PAWN_objective] the best way to lay the bricks to create sturdy structures.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CannibalChild.title: Cannibal child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CannibalChild.description: [PAWN_nameDef]’s tribe faced starvation when [PAWN_pronoun] was barely a year old. Desperate to keep their child alive, [PAWN_nameDef]‘s parents did what they felt they had to do, and fed [PAWN_objective] the flesh of a tribe member who had recently died.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_BoardGameMaster.title: Board game master
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_BoardGameMaster.description: [PAWN_nameDef] loved to play board games and was constantly begging the other children of [PAWN_possessive] village to play The Game of Ur with [PAWN_objective]. A kind craftsman noticed [PAWN_nameDef]’s passion, and showed [PAWN_objective] how to make [PAWN_possessive] own custom gameboard.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_Ward.title: Ward of the tribe
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_Ward.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was sent to live with another tribe in order to secure and strengthen an alliance. Because the alliance would fall apart if [PAWN_pronoun] was harmed, [PAWN_nameDef] was assigned the relatively safe job of helping to mind the cooking fires.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_PlagueSurvivor.title: Plague survivor
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_PlagueSurvivor.description: When plague struck [PAWN_nameDef]’s tribe, [PAWN_pronoun] was the only child to survive. [PAWN_pronoun] was quite lonely growing up, having no peers [PAWN_possessive] own age to talk to.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_RockCollector.title: Rock collector
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_RockCollector.description: [PAWN_nameDef] spent [PAWN_possessive] free time collecting different types of rocks and minerals. [PAWN_pronoun] sometimes made simple jewelry out of [PAWN_possessive] favourite stones by tying them with twine.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CuriousChild.title: Curious child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CuriousChild.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was naturally curious, constantly asking the adults of [PAWN_possessive] tribe questions about how things worked. Although this annoyed some, others were happy to teach [PAWN_nameDef] what [PAWN_pronoun] wanted to know.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_SpoiledChild.title: Spoiled child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_SpoiledChild.description: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up during a time of plenty in a family that was particularly wealthy. [PAWN_pronoun] enjoyed being the envy of the other children in the village, and frequently got into fights and arguments.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CaravanChild.title: Caravan child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CaravanChild.description: [PAWN_nameDef] frequently travelled with a trading caravan, helping the adults by tending to the pack animals. [PAWN_pronoun] got to see far-off places and met many interesting people.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_RatChaser.title: Rat chaser
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_RatChaser.description: [PAWN_nameDef] helped to chase away rats and other pests from [PAWN_possessive] village’s grain stores. [PAWN_pronoun] loved to play with the cats that hunted near the storage house.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CursedChild.title: Cursed child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_CursedChild.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was frequently sick as a child, coming close to death many times. [PAWN_possessive] family believed [PAWN_pronoun] had been hexed by an evil witch doctor, and tried all manner of rituals and prayers in order to remove the curse. [PAWN_nameDef] became somewhat good at identifying plants from constantly being around healing herbs.
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ChildSacrifice.title: Child sacrifice
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_ChildSacrifice.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was chosen to be one of the children who were annually sacrificed to [PAWN_possessive] tribe’s gods, something that was considered a great honour. However, when the time came, [PAWN_nameDef] ran away. [PAWN_pronoun] survived on roots and berries in the wild until a different tribe, who worshiped gods that did not demand death, found [PAWN_objective].
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_StolenChild.title: Stolen child
LanguageData.UB_Tribal_StolenChild.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was barely a toddler when [PAWN_pronoun] was kidnapped from [PAWN_possessive] settlement in a raid. The tribe who stole [PAWN_objective] believed that the advanced technology [PAWN_possessive] people had was magic, and that [PAWN_nameDef] was magic too. Although [PAWN_pronoun] did not manifest magical powers, the superior nutrition [PAWN_nameDef] received as an infant meant [PAWN_pronoun] was stronger than the other children in the tribe.
